resources.string: Stop hanging up. Let your AI answer.

Modes define the tone of the reply given to spammers before hanging up.

🤝 Polite — courteous and firm reply. Ideal for staying discreet.

📋 Administrative — cold, official tone, like a legal department.

😏 Sarcastic — humorous reply for telemarketers who deserve it.

🎭 Troll — makes the spammer wait 10 seconds before hanging up. Wastes their time.

Tap a mode to select it, then tap "Listen to a preview" to hear it.
Courteous but firm.\nIdeal for staying classy.
"This line does not accept commercial solicitations."
"Your call has been classified as unsolicited telemarketing."
For callers who really deserve it.
"Congratulations, you\'ve reached the most sarcastic voicemail in France."
Waste their time… a lot of time.
"Please hold, your call is very important to us."

Add your own blocking rules, on top of the automatic spam database:

• Exact number: blocks only this specific number (e.g. 0612345678).
• Prefix: blocks all numbers starting with this digit sequence (e.g. 0033612 blocks all numbers starting that way).

Any call matching a rule is rejected immediately, without a voice message.
Prefix — blocks all numbers starting this way
Basic protection stays free, forever. Premium adds personalization and comfort.

string-array.name: premium_features
string-array.item: 🚫 Manual blocking of numbers and prefixes
🇩🇪🇮🇹 Voice replies in German and Italian

resources.string: The subscription will be available at launch on the Google Play Store.
Activates Premium locally to test features, while waiting for the real subscription.
The phrases below are in %1$s, the language chosen for your voice messages. Select the ones that suit you.
How is your message put together?
Tu dégages automatically assembles a message in 3 parts before hanging up. You freely choose one option per section:
The opening line. Common to all modes.
The main reply. Varies depending on the active mode (%1$s).
The closing phrase. Common to all modes.
You can also create your own phrases at the bottom of each section.
The opening line spoken first by Tu dégages. Select the one that matches your style — only one at a time.
The heart of the reply, adapted to %1$s %2$s mode. This is the main phrase heard by the telemarketer. Only one at a time.
The closing phrase spoken right before hanging up. Only one at a time.

Log of all calls blocked by Tu dégages.

• Each row shows the detected number, date/time, mode used and message played.
• Use the filters at the top (All / Blocked / Replies / Manual) to sort the display.
• Tap the ✅ icon if this number was blocked by mistake: it will never be blocked again.
• Tap the 🗑️ icon to delete an entry from the history.
• Tap the ⬇️ icon to export the history as a CSV file (to save or send by email).
No voice found for this language.\nCheck your Android TTS settings.

Your anti-spam summary:

• The circular chart represents the time saved over 24h.
• Spammers blocked: total number of calls intercepted since installation.
• Calls avoided/day: daily average over the last 7 days.
• Average duration avoided: average length of a telemarketing call.
Every number you block is remembered
Update available in Settings → Update spam database
Why don\'t we share your data?
A phone number is personal data protected by GDPR. Sharing your list of blocked numbers with a third party — even to help other users — is forbidden without explicit consent.
That\'s why Tu dégages stores everything locally on your device and only consults official public sources (ARCEP, Signal-Spam). Your data never leaves your phone.
To report a number to the national community: signal-spam.fr
Tu dégages recognizes known telemarketing numbers and intercepts the call before your phone rings.
A synthetic voice answers the spammer for you, then hangs up. 4 tones available: polite, administrative, sarcastic or troll.
View the number of blocked calls, the time saved and the detailed history of each interaction.
Create your own messages, adjust the voice, speed and tone. Tu dégages adapts to your style.
Android will display a system popup titled:
"\"Set as default app for caller ID and spam protection\""
👉 Select \"Tu dégages\" from the list\n\n👉 Then tap \"Set as default\"
Without this authorization, Tu dégages cannot intercept telemarketing calls.
TU DÉGAGES, your new AI,\nwill love answering them!
Stop wasting time answering unwanted calls just to hang up afterwards. Let your personal AI answer with the humor of your choice.
TU DÉGAGES filters unwanted calls and redirects them to your personalized AI voice assistant — before ending the conversation. The telemarketer talks to your AI, not to you.
Classic apps just block calls. Ours lets your AI answer with the tone you choose — Polite, Administrative, Sarcastic or Troll — before hanging up.
Write your own phrases, choose the tone, adjust the voice. Your AI sounds like you. Spammers deserve a reply that matches. 🎤
Full history, time saved, numbers memorized for instant rejection. You\'ll know exactly how many times your AI has won.
Choose your country in Settings: the unwanted-number database adapts automatically (ARCEP prefixes in France, premium-rate and OFCOM business numbers in Switzerland).
You can review this presentation at any time from Settings → Review presentation.

Configure how Tu dégages behaves:

• Protection: enables/disables global call filtering.
• Auto-answer: answers the call without making the phone ring (recommended).
• Block after reply: adds the number to the blacklist after each interaction.
• Notifications: alerts you for every blocked call.
• Customize replies: create and manage your own messages.
• Voice settings: change the voice, speed and pitch.

This setting only changes the language of the interface (menus, buttons, app screens). It has no effect on the language spoken to telemarketers — for that, see "Voice message language" below. Choose "System" so the app automatically follows your phone\'s language.

The selected country adapts the unwanted-number database and the phone number formats monitored for automatic spam detection. Choose "Switzerland" if you mostly receive calls from Swiss telemarketers.

This is the language Tu dégages speaks aloud to telemarketers during a screened call. It\'s independent from the app\'s language: you can have the app in French while voice messages are in German. German, Italian and English are Premium-only.

"Protection" is the master switch: when off, Tu dégages lets every call ring normally without filtering anything. "Auto-answer" answers suspicious calls without making your phone ring. "Block number after reply" adds the number to your blacklist after replying to it, so it gets rejected instantly next time. "Notifications" alerts you for every blocked call.

When this option is on, any call without a displayed number (hidden, private, or unknown) is rejected immediately and silently, with no voice message. Useful against unwanted anonymous calls, but be careful: some legitimate callers (doctors, schools…) sometimes call with a hidden number.
Turns on the speakerphone while the voice message is played to the telemarketer, so you can hear live what Tu dégages says. Useful to check everything works, but can be awkward in public.

This section lets you customize the exact content of the voice messages played to telemarketers: the greeting, the body (different for each mode — Polite/Administrative/Sarcastic/Troll) and the closing line. Pick from several preset sentences or write your own.

Adjust the speed, pitch and voice Tu dégages uses to speak to telemarketers. The list of available voices depends on the language chosen in "Voice message language" and the voices installed on your phone.

Add numbers or prefixes here that you want to block systematically, in addition to the automatic spam database. Ideal for a specific annoying number, or a whole prefix (e.g. a call center\'s area code).
Premium unlocks German/Italian/English voice messages, full customization of replies, voice selection, custom number blocking, and contributing to the community spam database.

Turning on contribution (the "Community database" toggle above) anonymously reports every number Tu dégages blocks on your phone. In return, your app regularly downloads new numbers reported by other users to grow your own blocklist.
Adapts the unwanted-number database to your country.
Language in which Tu dégages replies to telemarketers (useful in German- or Italian-speaking Switzerland).
Automatically rejects, without a voice message, calls without a displayed number (hidden, private, unknown).
🔊 Listen to the AI reply live
Hear what your AI says to the spammer through your speaker

Every spammer blocked by a user is reported to the whole community. Each time it connects, your app automatically downloads new numbers reported by all users — and adds them to your local database. The more users, the more formidable the database becomes against spammers.
Only the spam caller\'s number is transmitted — never your personal data.

Tu dégages protects your phone against telemarketing calls.

• The ON/OFF button enables or disables protection.
• The counters show your blocking statistics.
• The list at the bottom shows the most recent blocked calls.
The AI that answers your spammers
🤖 The smart spam-blocking app with AI replies
TU DÉGAGES, your new AI, will love answering them!
❌ Other apps just block the call.
Your AI answers for you.\nSpammers no longer get through.
No blocked calls yet\nEnable protection and wait… 😏

🆓 FREE, FOREVER — protection:

• Automatic blocking of known spam (ARCEP/OFCOM database + learning from your history)
• Polite reply mode
• History of blocked calls
• Your contacts are never blocked
• "Not spam": fix a wrong block in one tap
• Notifications for every blocked call

Security and protection against false positives will NEVER be paid.

⭐ PREMIUM (€1/month or €10/year) — comfort and personalization:

• Administrative, Sarcastic and Troll modes
• Full message customization (builder, custom phrases)
• Voice settings (voice, speed, pitch)
• Community database (sync and contribution)
• Manual blocking of numbers and prefixes
• CSV export of history
• Switzerland mode (OFCOM numbers)
• Voice replies in German and Italian

A 🔒 lock appears on every Premium feature in the app. Tap it to see the offer details in Settings → "⭐ Tu dégages Premium".
👤 Can my contacts be blocked by mistake?

No. If a number calling you matches a contact saved on your phone, Tu dégages always lets the call through — even if that number is also in the spam database or in your custom rules.\n\nThis check requires contacts access permission, requested the first time you launch the app.
📞 I\'m waiting for a callback from an unknown number (order, quote...): could it get blocked?

The risk is low: an unknown number is only blocked if it's in the spam database (known prefixes, community reports) or in one of your manual rules — not a typical customer service or delivery number.

If the call still gets blocked, it remains visible in the History tab with the number and time: you can call back, then tap ✅ "Not spam" so it's never blocked again.

If you're expecting an important call and want zero risk, temporarily disable protection from the home screen during the call, then re-enable it afterwards.

Tu dégages automatically intercepts calls from known telemarketing numbers, plays them a voice message, then hangs up — without your phone ever ringing.\n\nYou save time and energy by never having to pick up to hear a robot offer you a fitted kitchen.
🎯 Why don\'t we block \"an entire carrier\"?

Some competing apps claim to block, in bulk, numbers belonging to VoIP carriers (Aircall, Vonage, Manifone, Tata Communications, CM Telecom, etc.).

Tu dégages made the opposite choice, and here's why:

These carriers are general-purpose VoIP providers, not "spam carriers". They are used:
❌ by telemarketing call centers,
✅ but also by legitimate businesses: customer support, deliveries, medical appointments, tradespeople, etc.

If we blocked "all of Aircall" or "all of Vonage", we'd also block these legitimate calls — a big risk of false positives and complaints ("why can't my delivery driver reach me?!").

The right approach is the one we built: the community database (see below) captures numbers actually reported as spam by users, regardless of the carrier behind them — without ever blocking legitimate uses of the same carrier.

Tu dégages prefers precise, reliable blocking rather than a blunt rejection made to impress.
🔁 A number calls me back several times in a short period: what happens?

This is a common technique used by telemarketing call centers: they call back repeatedly if you don't answer, sometimes from numbers not (yet) known as spam.

Tu dégages automatically detects these bursts: if the same unknown number calls you 3 times or more within 30 minutes, it is automatically added to your personal spam database and all its subsequent calls are silently rejected.

This mechanism only applies to numbers calling in bursts — a single call from a delivery driver, doctor or relative is never affected. And as always, if a number is blocked by mistake, the ✅ "Not spam" icon in History lets you unblock it in one tap.
1. Open Tu dégages.\n2. Make sure the protection switch is ON (green) on the home screen.\n3. That\'s it. Tu dégages works in the background.

Go to the "Modes" tab to choose the tone of the reply:

🤝 Polite — courteous and firm reply. Ideal if you prefer to stay discreet.

📋 Administrative — cold, official tone, like a legal department. Throws off call center operators.

😏 Sarcastic — humorous reply. For telemarketers who deserve a bit of irony.

🎭 Troll — makes the spammer wait through 10 seconds of hold music before hanging up. Wastes their time and money.

In Settings → Customize replies, you can:

• Enable or disable each existing phrase.
• Add your own custom phrases.
• Use the Message Builder to compose a message in 3 parts: greeting + body + closing.

In Settings → Voice settings:

• Choose the voice (depending on the voices installed on your phone).
• Adjust the speech rate (from slow to fast).
• Change the pitch of the voice (low or high).
• Test the result with the preview button.
🇫🇷🇨🇭 Does Tu dégages work in Switzerland?

Yes! In Settings, choose your country (🇫🇷 France or 🇨🇭 Switzerland) at the top of the list.

In Switzerland, Tu dégages notably detects:

• Premium-rate numbers (0900, 0901, 0906) — business, marketing and entertainment, individually assigned by OFCOM.
• National shared-cost business numbers (0840, 0842, 0844, 0848, 0878) — often used as redirect numbers by call centers and commercial hotlines.

The community database and your personal history work exactly the same way, regardless of the chosen country.
🇩🇪🇮🇹 Can it reply in German or Italian (other regions of Switzerland)?

Yes. In Settings, just below the country selector, choose the "Voice message language": 🇫🇷 Français, 🇩🇪 Deutsch or 🇮🇹 Italiano.

In the chosen language, Tu dégages plays a fixed message adapted to the active mode (Polite, Administrative, Sarcastic or Troll), with a matching voice if your phone offers one.

This setting does not change the language of the app itself, which stays in your chosen interface language — only the message heard by the telemarketer changes.

Tu dégages combines several sources:

• Documented ARCEP ranges: 52 French prefixes officially assigned to telemarketing.
• phoneblock.net: open-source European community database.
• Signal-Spam France: French anti-spam association.
• Tu dégages community database: numbers reported by users (opt-in).
• Your personal history: any number that has called you and been treated as spam.
What is the purpose of reporting a number?

When you block a number and have enabled "Community database" in Settings, that number is sent — anonymously, without any of your data — to a shared server.

All other Tu dégages users then retrieve this number and automatically reject it, without even playing the voice message. The more users there are, the more effective the database is for everyone.

This retrieval happens automatically, silently, once a day when the app is opened — you don't have to do anything. The "Update spam database" button in Settings simply forces an immediate update.

A number is blocked if it belongs to a known telemarketing ARCEP range, if it appears in phoneblock.net or Signal-Spam, if it has already called you and been treated as spam, or if it has been reported by the community.\n\nTu dégages never blocks a number simply because it belongs to a particular telecom carrier — see \"Why don\'t we block an entire carrier\" above.
🚫 Can I block a number or prefix myself?

Yes! In Settings → Manually blocked numbers, you can add your own rules:

• Exact number: blocks only this specific number (e.g. 0612345678).
• Prefix: blocks all numbers starting with this digit sequence (e.g. 0033612 blocks all numbers starting that way).

These rules are added on top of the automatic spam database: any matching call is rejected immediately, without a voice message. You can remove a rule at any time from the same screen.

The History tab lists all blocked calls:

• Detected phone number.
• Date and time of the block.
• Mode used to reply.
• Exact message played to the caller.

Use the filters (All / Blocked / Replies / Manual) to refine the display. Delete an entry with the 🗑️ icon.
⬇️ Can I download the list of blocked numbers?

Yes. In the History tab, tap the ⬇️ icon at the top of the screen: a CSV file (number, date, mode, message played) is generated and you can save it or send it by email via the Android share menu.\n\nThis export is purely personal: it serves as a record or proof, for example for an official report. It sends nothing to Tu dégages or anyone else.

The Statistics tab shows:

• Total time saved (circular chart).
• Total number of spammers blocked since installation.
• Average calls avoided per day.
• Average duration of a telemarketing call avoided.

In Settings:

• Auto-answer: Tu dégages answers the call without making your phone ring (recommended).
• Block after reply: adds the number to your personal blacklist after each interaction.
• Notifications: if enabled, you receive an Android notification for every blocked call (number and reason for blocking).

Tu dégages adds a tile to Android's quick settings panel (the one you open by swiping down with two fingers from the top of the screen, next to Wi-Fi and Bluetooth).

To add it: open the quick settings panel, tap the pencil ✏️ (or "Edit"), then drag the "Tu dégages" tile to your active tiles.

Tapping the tile instantly enables or disables protection, without needing to open the app.
What to do if a number is blocked by mistake?

Go to the History tab, find the call in question and tap the ✅ "Not spam" icon.

This number is then immediately removed from your spam database and custom rules, and added to your local whitelist: it will never be blocked again on your phone, even if a spam database update reintroduces it.

No email to send, no waiting: everything happens directly on your phone, in your local database — it works even without an internet connection and takes effect from the next call.

If you're expecting an important call from an unknown number, you can also temporarily disable protection (home screen) for the duration of the call.
What if I\'m a victim of spoofing or number impersonation?

Spoofing (a spammer displaying a fake number, sometimes yours or a relative\'s) is a phone network issue that Tu dégages cannot fix: the app only sees the number displayed by the carrier.\n\nIf you receive calls showing your own number or a spoofed number, reporting it to your carrier or to 33700 (anti-fraud SMS/call service in France) remains the most effective solution.
A spam number is not blocked, what should I do?

1. Check that protection is enabled (home screen).
2. Check that Tu dégages is set as the default caller ID app (Android Settings → Apps → Default apps → Caller ID and spam app).
3. Update the spam database manually: Settings → Update spam database.
4. If the number keeps calling after that, it will be remembered after the first block and rejected automatically next time.

No. Tu dégages plays its own message to the caller and then hangs up — the caller does not have access to your usual voicemail for this call.\n\nThis is intentional: a telemarketer who can neither talk to you nor leave a message has much less reason to call back.

If Tu dégages isn't blocking calls, check:

1. Protection is set to ON (home screen).
2. Tu dégages is set as the call screening service: Android Settings → Apps → Default apps → Caller ID and spam app → Select Tu dégages.
3. Phone permissions are granted to Tu dégages.
Does Tu dégages use a lot of battery?

No. Tu dégages doesn\'t run continuously in the background: Android only wakes the app at the exact moment a call comes in, via the system\'s call screening service.\n\nOutside of calls, the app is completely inactive. The only network activity happens when the spam database is updated (manually or when the app opens).

By default, Tu dégages collects and transmits no data: everything stays on your phone.\n\nIf you enable the community database (opt-in), only the spam caller\'s number is sent to a server hosted in Europe — never your number or personal information. Full details are in About → Privacy policy.
How do I report a bug or give feedback?
You can write to us directly at the contact address shown in About. Describe the issue (phone model, Android version, what happened) — it helps us a lot to improve Tu dégages.
